steps.title: Determine what scale factor k must satisfy
steps.content: Side of T = k × side of S. For T's side to be **greater** than S's side, we need k > 1.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice numerically
steps.content:
A) $\frac{29}{28} \approx 1.036 > 1$ — T's sides are longer than S's ✓
B) $1$ — sides are equal, not greater
C) $\frac{28}{29} \approx 0.966 < 1$ — T's sides would be shorter
D) $0$ — sides collapse to length zero

steps.title: Select the valid value
steps.content: Only $\frac{29}{28}$ exceeds 1. That matches choice A — A is correct.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '29/28': $\frac{29}{28} \approx 1.036 > 1$, so each side of T is strictly longer than the corresponding side of S. ✓
  • Choice B — '1': k = 1 produces a congruent triangle — sides are equal, not greater. ✗
  • Choice C — '28/29': $\frac{28}{29} \approx 0.966 < 1$, so each side of T would be shorter than S's sides. ✗
  • Choice D — '0': k = 0 collapses every side to length 0 — not a valid triangle. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
